articles.headline: Lynx rookie Miles reaches 400 points, 100 rebounds, 100 assists
articles.description: Minnesota rookie Olivia Miles scored a career-high 33, becoming the first WNBA player to reach 400 points, 100 rebounds and 100 assists in only 22 career games, and the Lynx defeated the Phoenix Mercury 104-100 Monday night for a third straight victory.

articles.description: Entering the week at 17-7, the Golden State Valkyries now have the fourth-shortest odds to win the WNBA title.

articles.headline: Fever beat Aces again as Caitlin Clark scores 12 in 24 minutes
articles.description: The Fever beat the defending champion Aces in Las Vegas for the second time in eight days. Caitlin Clark, who has been dealing with back issues, played 24 minutes and finished with 12 points, seven rebounds and six assists.

articles.description: — Caitlin Clark had 12 points, seven rebounds and six assists, Kelsey Mitchell scored 27 points, and the Indiana Fever beat A'ja Wilson and the Las Vegas Aces 109-75 on Sunday night.

article.headline: Kayla McBride, Olivia Miles star in the Lynx's 104-100 win over the Mercury
article.description: — Kayla McBride scored a season-high 37 points, rookie Olivia Miles had a career-high 33 and the Minnesota Lynx beat the Phoenix Mercury 104-100 on Monday night for their third straight victory.
article.linkText: Kayla McBride, Olivia Miles star in the Lynx's 104-100 win over the Mercury

images.name: Sami Whitcomb knocks down the 3-pointer
images.caption: Sami Whitcomb knocks down the 3-pointer

tracking.trackingName: WNBA_One-Play (Sami Whitcomb knocks down the 3-pointer) 2026/07/14 ESHEET

article.story: MINNEAPOLIS -- — Kayla McBride scored a season-high 37 points, rookie Olivia Miles had a career-high 33 and the Minnesota Lynx beat the Phoenix Mercury 104-100 on Monday night for their third straight victory. McBride's sixth 3-pointer gave Minnesota a 101-98 lead with 45.1 seconds left. Miles also had eight assists. Miles drove into the lane and found Natasha Howard for a layup with 21.1 seconds left to make it 103-100. Courtney Williams had a steal at the other end, and Miles made 1 of 2 free throws for a four-point lead. The game featured 23 lead changes and 13 ties — with both teams shooting 55% from the field. Minnesota (18-6) made 24 of 26 free throws. McBride finished one point shy of her career high set in 2018. Miles became the fastest player in WNBA history to reach 400 points, 100 rebounds and 100 assists — doing it in 22 games. Kahleah Copper scored 26 points, and Alyssa Thomas had 19 points, eight rebounds and 12 assists for Phoenix (8-17). Up next Mercury: Host Connecticut on Friday and Sunday. Lynx: Host Los Angeles on Wednesday. ------ AP WNBA: https://apnews.com/hub/wnba-basketball
